data.title: Santiment Warns of Contrarian Signal Amid Bitcoin's Social Media Optimism
data.description: Santiment has issued a warning regarding the prevalent optimism on social media platforms about Bitcoin's potential recovery to $90,000. According to NS3.AI, the firm noted that bullish sentiments were widespread across platforms like X, Reddit, and Telegram last week, overshadowing bearish perspectives, which were often dismissed as fear, uncertainty, and doubt (FUD).

data.title: Meta Platforms to Offer Prepaid Mobile Recharge Service to WhatsApp Users in India
data.description: Meta Platforms has announced plans to introduce a prepaid mobile recharge service for WhatsApp users in India. According to Jin10, this initiative aims to enhance the functionality of WhatsApp by integrating mobile recharge capabilities directly within the app. This move is part of Meta's broader strategy to expand its services and increase user engagement in one of its largest markets. The service will allow users to conveniently recharge their mobile phones without leaving the WhatsApp platform, streamlining the process and providing added convenience.

data.title: SignalPlus participated in the Hong Kong Web3 Carnival as the title sponsor.
data.description: During the Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, SignalPlus, an institutional-grade digital asset derivatives trading infrastructure platform, participated as the title sponsor and set up a booth in the core area of ​​the main venue. SignalPlus highlighted its product capabilities in derivatives execution and risk management, and exchanged ideas with many institutional traders and industry professionals.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot Trading Volume Hits Lowest Level Since October 2023
data.description: Bitcoin spot trading volume across major exchanges has reached its lowest level since October 2023, according to Foresight News. Data from Glassnode indicates that this decline in trading volume is typically associated with reduced market depth and increased sensitivity to changes in capital flows.

data.title: B.AI and deBridge Collaborate on Cross-Chain Infrastructure for AI Economy
data.description: B.AI has announced a collaboration with deBridge to explore cross-chain infrastructure aimed at enhancing the scalability of the AI Agent economy. According to ChainCatcher, this partnership will focus on seamless cross-chain execution. B.AI is dedicated to developing financial infrastructure for the AI Agent era, while deBridge's zero-TVL model and gas-free cross-chain technology provide a secure and decentralized foundation. Utilizing deBridge's MCP and intent-driven Bundles, the collaboration aims to advance an efficient and secure autonomous trading system, accelerating cross-chain execution in the AI Agent era.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Singapore's Gold Imports from Dubai Reach Record High
data.description: Singapore's gold imports from Dubai reached an unprecedented level last month, marking a significant milestone in the trade relationship between the two regions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the surge in gold imports as a reflection of increasing demand and strategic trade partnerships. The record-breaking import figures underscore Singapore's growing role as a key player in the global gold market, driven by its strategic location and robust financial infrastructure. This development comes amid fluctuating global gold prices and shifting economic dynamics, positioning Singapore as a pivotal hub for precious metals trading.

data.title: BNB's Institutional Investment Logic Discussed at Hong Kong Web3 Carnival
data.description: During the Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, Hash Global founder KK elaborated on the investment logic of BNB as an institutional-grade asset. According to PANews, KK's speech focused on the potential of BNB in institutional portfolios, highlighting its strategic value in the evolving digital asset landscape.

data.title: Brent Oil Futures Surge Ahead of U.S. Stock Market Opening
data.description:
Brent oil futures experienced a significant surge ahead of the U.S. stock market opening, reaching a high of $108, according to ChainCatcher. Hyperinsight monitoring revealed that the BRENTOIL platform saw a 24-hour increase of 3%, with prices recovering to levels seen before the sharp decline earlier this month. The recent gains have nearly offset the losses incurred over the past month.

A major BRENTOIL short position holder faced forced liquidation during this upward trend, with a total liquidation scale of $2.75 million and a recorded loss of approximately $250,000. This short position was initially opened on April 24.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks were mixed in pre-market trading in the US, with HOOD down 10.08%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks generally rose in pre-market trading in the US, with COIN down 1.08%; MSTR up 1.47%, ABTC up 1.71%, HOOD down 10.08%, MARA up 0.64%, and CRCL up 0.40%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Resistance Amid Surge in Exchange Inflows
data.description:
Bitcoin's net inflow to centralized exchanges (CEX) has surged to 9,905 BTC, marking the largest single-day inflow in the past 30 days, according to ChainCatcher. This influx has prevented Bitcoin from effectively breaking through resistance levels.

CryptoQuant analyst Woominkyu highlighted concerns over the Exchange Whale Ratio, which spiked to 0.707 on April 27, the highest in over a week. This indicates that more than 70% of the inflow transactions are dominated by the top 10 entries, suggesting that large holders are actively transferring tokens to CEX, possibly in preparation for distribution.

Woominkyu believes that the combination of whale-driven inflow and rising CEX reserves suggests that smart money is preparing to sell at higher prices. Unless this inflow is quickly absorbed, the likelihood of retesting the $74,000 to $75,000 support zone in the short term is significantly increasing.

data.title: Celsius Founder Reaches Settlement with FTC, Faces Permanent Ban
data.description:
Celsius founder Alexander Mashinsky has reached a settlement with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC), according to Foresight News. The agreement permanently prohibits him from promoting products or services related to asset deposits, exchanges, investments, or withdrawals. Additionally, Mashinsky is required to pay $10 million.

A ruling signed by Judge Denise Cote of the Southern District of New York on Tuesday indicates that the FTC's $4.72 billion judgment against Mashinsky is largely suspended. However, this suspension could be lifted if Mashinsky fails to disclose significant assets, misreports asset values, or omits crucial information in his financial disclosures, at which point the judgment amount would become immediately due.

In May 2025, Mashinsky was sentenced to 12 years in prison after admitting to charges of commodities fraud and securities fraud.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Downside as Kevin Warsh Prepares to Lead Federal Reserve
data.description: Bitcoin may experience a period of decline in the coming months as Kevin Warsh is set to assume the role of U.S. Federal Reserve chair next month. According to NS3.AI, U.S. President Donald Trump expressed that he "would" be disappointed if Warsh does not implement rate cuts at his first Federal Open Market Committee meeting in June.

data.title: Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky has settled with the FTC and will pay $10 million.
data.description: Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky has reached a settlement with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC), agreeing to pay $10 million and be permanently banned from promoting products or services involving the deposit, exchange, investment, or withdrawal of assets. Under the court order, the enforcement of most of the FTC's previous $4.72 billion damages judgment has been suspended, but the judgment amount can be reinstated if Mashinsky's financial disclosures contain material omissions or false statements. Previously, Mashinsky was sentenced to 12 years in prison in 2025 for commodity fraud and securities fraud. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Moonrock Capital Founder Criticizes TRUMP Meme Coin's Impact on Crypto Regulation
data.description: Moonrock Capital founder Simon Dedic has expressed concerns over the TRUMP meme coin, labeling it as a significant hindrance to establishing a clear regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, Dedic believes that Democrats are leveraging the situation to introduce ethics clauses into the Clarity Act, potentially delaying or obstructing the bill's progress in Congress.

data.title: ZetaChain Reports Exploit in Cross-Chain Messaging Pipeline
data.description: ZetaChain has disclosed an exploit in its cross-chain messaging pipeline, resulting in losses amounting to $333,868 and impacting its GatewayEVM contract. According to NS3.AI, the company assured that no user funds were compromised, with only three internal team wallets affected across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, and BSC. ZetaChain has implemented a mainnet patch to address the issue, although cross-chain transactions will remain suspended until further upgrades and reviews are finalized.

data.title: QCP: Markets shift to cautious wait-and-see mode; Bitcoin remains range-bound.
data.description: QCP released a market watchlist stating that as geopolitical gains fade, macroeconomic uncertainty has once again dominated market sentiment, with both traditional and crypto markets adopting a cautious wait-and-see approach. The report points out that the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and Powell's remarks may be key short-term variables; a hawkish signal could put pressure on liquidity-driven assets. QCP also notes that Bitcoin has maintained range-bound trading after its April rally, with current positions being relatively light and volatility compressed, reflecting the market's overall wait-and-see phase before a directional move.

data.title: BIT: Bitcoin rises for the second consecutive month, with signs of market momentum recovery.
data.description: BIT released a chart analysis today, noting that Bitcoin weakened for five consecutive months after reaching its all-time high in October 2025, before recording positive returns in March to end the previous downtrend. April is expected to see its strongest monthly gain since April 2025, marking its second consecutive month of gains. The analysis suggests that two consecutive months of gains indicate a recovery, showing improved market sentiment and price momentum. If this is combined with the seasonally strong performance from May to July, Bitcoin's price action could receive further support.

data.title: Market Sentiment Shifts to Caution Amid Geopolitical Changes
data.description:
QCP has released a market observation indicating that as geopolitical benefits fade, macroeconomic uncertainty is once again dominating market sentiment. According to Odaily, both traditional and crypto markets are adopting a cautious stance. The report highlights that the Federal Reserve's meeting and statements from U.S. President Donald Trump could be key short-term variables. If hawkish signals are released, it may exert pressure on liquidity-driven assets.

QCP also noted that after Bitcoin's rise in April, it has maintained a range-bound movement. Current positions are light, and volatility is compressed, reflecting the market's overall state of waiting for a directional choice.

data.title: Belgium's GDP Growth in First Quarter
data.description: Belgium's GDP grew by 0.2% quarter-on-quarter and 0.8% year-on-year in the first quarter, according to Jin10.
